enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Chinna Paappa?

The song Chinna Paappa conveys the innocence and joy of childhood, celebrating the playful nature of children.

In which movie does Chinna Paappa appear?

Chinna Paappa is featured in the movie Vannakili.

Who is the singer of Chinna Paappa?

The song is sung by the legendary singer P. Susheela.

Who composed the music for Chinna Paappa?

The music for Chinna Paappa was composed by K.V. Mahadevan.

When was Chinna Paappa released?

Chinna Paappa was released in the year 1970.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Chinna Paappa from Vannakili beautifully encapsulates the carefree spirit of childhood. It portrays a playful interaction, filled with joy and innocence, making it a cherished melody for many. The lyrics evoke a sense of nostalgia, reminding listeners of their own childhood experiences. With its soothing tune and heartfelt delivery, this song resonates deeply, making it a timeless classic.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Chinna Paappa from Vannakili is a delightful melody that captures the essence of childhood joy and innocence. With P. Susheela's enchanting vocals and K.V. Mahadevan's timeless composition, this song evokes a sense of nostalgia that resonates with listeners of all ages. Experience the charm of this classic as it takes you on a heartwarming journey down memory lane.
stanzaMeanings: Little baby, our dear baby, when you speak, it sounds good.
Little baby, our dear baby, when you speak, it sounds good.
Will you bring me some sweet candy to eat? Do you want a small toy balloon?
Will I tell you a fun game? Then you will laugh and see what I do.
Little baby, our dear baby, when you speak, it sounds good.
When you get angry, your father will call you. When you speak softly, they will feed you.
The chicken is pecking at your little face, don't let it go.
I will give you ripe fruit, don't cry, little baby, our dear baby.
